column1: # Active poll rows remain disabled to keep the bus passive-only.
# `ebus_reset` is the standard bus reset write entry kept for normal ebusd compatibility.
# `boiler_operating_mode` is the main high-level operating state of the boiler.
# The value map in the row is kept intentionally explicit because it is one of the most useful production entities.
# `dhw_actual_temperature` is the measured domestic hot water temperature.
# `dhw_eco_setpoint` is the confirmed DHW eco target.
# `dhw_setpoint` is the confirmed writable DHW target register.
# `dhw_setpoint_bc` is the passive broadcast mirror of the same value.
# - `primary_setpoint`: the dynamic heating target seen changing with operating state
# - `circuit_setpoint`: the circuit target carried in the same frame
# - `dhw_reference`: the DHW-related reference value carried in the same frame
# `flame_target_temperature` is the internal flow/flame target temperature used by the controller.
# `max_heating_setpoint` is the confirmed maximum heating setpoint.
# `internal_flow_target_temperature` is the controller internal flow target.
# `main_flow_temperature` is the main heating flow temperature leaving the boiler.
# `aux_probe_temperature` is an embedded temperature frame with two internal bytes skipped before the real value.
# `secondary_return_temperature` is another embedded temperature frame with internal bytes skipped before the real value.
# `outdoor_temperature_bc` is valid when an outdoor probe exists; `3276.7` usually means raw `ff7f`
# `pump_modulation` is the pump modulation level reported by the boiler.
# `pressure_sensor_voltage` is the raw pressure sensor voltage exposed as volts.
# `zone1_room_temperature` is the confirmed room temperature reported for zone 1.
# `zone1_current_setpoint_bc` is the current heating setpoint requested by zone 1.
# `zone1_day_temperature_bc` and `zone1_night_temperature_bc` are the most useful scalar reads from a structured `200e` setpoint family.
# Dumps still show that `6271` can also appear as a full triple (`23.0 / 10.0 / 30.0`) during heating-related captures
column2: so treat these rows as practical mirrors

column1: # `zone1_setpoint_bundle_bc` is the confirmed bundle of antifreeze
# `zone1_heat_request_raw` is a confirmed raw request marker. It is kept raw on purpose because the exact semantics are still byte-level.
# `heating_active_bc` is the confirmed passive `0120` status field from `03/fe/2000`.
# Dumps show `0120` belongs to a wider grouped status/request family
column2: so keep it as a raw status-like field rather than a pure boolean sensor.

column1: # `request_status_1` and `request_status_2` are confirmed heating-related status bytes from the 2010 family.
# `heating_flame_mode_bc` is a confirmed logical heating-flame indicator; it should not be confused with physical burner flame proof.
# `zone1_request_mode_bc` is the confirmed request-mode code reported with the zone setpoint family.
# `heating_active_short_bc` exposes the short `200f` packet carrying the same `0120` field.
# Keep it raw as well: dump evidence does not support a stable boolean interpretation for the wider `0120/0f23/1023` family.
# `zone1_thermoreg_limits_bc` is the confirmed live thermoreg limits bundle for the main zone.
# `thermoreg_profile73_limits_bc` and `thermoreg_profile75_limits_bc` are confirmed parallel thermoreg limit bundles observed live.
# End of production file. Any candidate rows
column4: or partially decoded bundles live in `aris-development.csv`.
